title: Mapinguari cacaoensis

This family is part of the order Malpighiales, a group of flowering plants that are primarily found in the Americas. properties: Mapinguari cacaoensis, also known as the "giant armadillo," is not typically associated with the plant cacaoensis, but rather the animal. However, there is a plant called Theobroma cacao, which is a source of cacao, and it has been reported to possess various therapeutic properties.
